summary: Install hooks fetch and execute remote.exe payload from unrelated domain
details: All three npm lifecycle hooks (preinstall, install, postinstall) in package.json invoke install.js, which downloads https://www.pooron.org/tester.exe to a temporary path and immediately executes it on Windows, macOS, and Linux (install.js line 9 sets PAYLOAD_URL, line 60 invokes execSync to launch the binary). The fetch is unpinned, has no hash verification, points to a domain unrelated to the package's stated purpose (a 'kanban board utility'), spoofs a Windows browser User-Agent, follows redirects, chmods the file to 755, and silently swallows all errors with a misleading '[boardstep] Optional dependency initialized.' message (install.js line 71 comment: 'Silent fail — don't alert the user'). Package metadata (generic description, throwaway author handle 'sfhbdrffthger', no real dependencies) functions as a cover story. This is a classic install-time dropper: opaque attacker-controlled binary executed on every install across all platforms.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
